files_changed.summary: Core OAuth2 client — builds authorization URLs, exchanges codes for tokens, fetches user profiles from Google and GitHub APIs
Token management — stores, retrieves, and refreshes OAuth access/refresh tokens with encrypted-at-rest storage
Sign-in route handler — generates state parameter, builds OAuth URL, redirects to provider
Callback route handler — validates state, exchanges code for tokens, creates/links user account, creates session
Sign-out route handler — destroys session, clears cookie, redirects to home
Extended with OAuth session data fields (provider, access_token_ref) and profile-linking lookup
Added OAuthAccount model linked to User, storing provider, provider_account_id, access_token, refresh_token, expires_at
Added auth check — redirects unauthenticated users, displays user profile for authenticated users
Database migration adding oauth_accounts table with unique constraint on (provider, provider_account_id)

functions_added.name: buildAuthorizationUrl
functions_added.purpose: Constructs the OAuth2 authorization URL with correct scopes and redirect URI for the given provider

functions_added.name: exchangeCodeForTokens
functions_added.purpose: Exchanges an authorization code for access and refresh tokens via the provider's token endpoint

functions_added.name: fetchUserProfile
functions_added.purpose: Fetches the user's profile (email, name, avatar) from the provider's API using the access token

functions_added.name: validateOAuthCallback
functions_added.signature: validateOAuthCallback(params: { state: string; code: string; storedState: string }): boolean
functions_added.purpose: Validates the callback parameters — checks state matches and code is present. Returns boolean, never throws.

functions_added.name: storeTokens
functions_added.signature: storeTokens(userId: string, provider: string, tokens: OAuthTokens): Promise<void>
functions_added.purpose: Encrypts and stores OAuth tokens in the database, linked to the user's OAuthAccount record

functions_added.name: refreshAccessToken
functions_added.purpose: Refreshes an expired access token using the stored refresh token. Returns the new access token.

functions_added.name: findOrCreateUser
functions_added.purpose: Finds existing user by email for profile linking or creates new user. Links the OAuth account to the user.

unit_tests_written.summary: Tests cover URL construction for both providers, code exchange with mocked HTTP, profile fetching, state validation, token storage/retrieval, and findOrCreateUser with existing and new users.

coverage_gaps: Race condition when two concurrent refreshAccessToken calls for the same user execute simultaneously — both may try to use the same refresh token, and if the provider invalidates the old token on first use, the second call will fail with an invalid_grant error
Token encryption key rotation — storeTokens encrypts with the current key but there's no test for reading tokens encrypted with a previous key after rotation
Profile linking when Google and GitHub return slightly different email formats (e.g., 'User@Gmail.com' vs 'user@gmail.com') — normalization exists but only tested with lowercase
Network timeout handling in exchangeCodeForTokens — the fetch has a 10s timeout but no test simulates a slow provider response
Behavior when OAuth provider returns valid tokens but the profile endpoint returns a 403 (revoked permissions between token grant and profile fetch)

properties_believed.property: validateOAuthCallback always returns boolean, never throws, for any combination of inputs including null/undefined
properties_believed.evidence: try-catch wraps the entire function body; 12 unit tests with valid, invalid, null, undefined, and empty string inputs all pass

properties_believed.property: findOrCreateUser is idempotent — calling it twice with the same profile returns the same user ID
properties_believed.evidence: Uses upsert with email as unique key; tested with duplicate calls in session.test.ts

properties_believed.property: refreshAccessToken is safe to call concurrently for different users
properties_believed.evidence: Each call queries by userId+provider, no shared mutable state, but no concurrent test exists

properties_believed.property: Token encryption uses AES-256-GCM with unique IV per encryption, making stored tokens secure at rest
properties_believed.evidence: Implementation uses crypto.randomBytes(16) for IV but no test verifies two encryptions of the same value produce different ciphertexts

properties_believed.property: The state parameter in OAuth flow is cryptographically random and single-use
properties_believed.evidence: Uses crypto.randomBytes(32) for generation, but state cleanup after use is time-based (5 min expiry) not immediate deletion — a replay within 5 minutes is theoretically possible

known_risks: State parameter replay window: 5-minute expiry means a captured state could theoretically be reused within that window, though the authorization code itself is single-use at the provider level
Prisma transaction isolation: findOrCreateUser uses a transaction but the default isolation level may allow phantom reads under high concurrency
No rate limiting on the callback endpoint — a malicious actor could spam invalid codes to trigger rate limits on the provider's token endpoint, potentially locking out legitimate auth attempts
GitHub API occasionally returns 502 during high load — the retry logic uses exponential backoff but only 2 retries, which may not be sufficient during GitHub incidents
integration_points: PostgreSQL via Prisma ORM (user table, oauth_accounts table, session table)
Node.js crypto module for token encryption and state generation
